modified_files.change: Added declared dependency "python-dotenv>=1.0" to [project].dependencies, with an explanatory comment tying it to issue #28 subtask 5.3.5 and clarifying the security boundary is unchanged.

regression_surface: Zero. Diff only adds two new files under agents/eval/ and one additive dependency-list entry in agents/pyproject.toml; no existing production or test file is modified.
risk_notes: run_live_benchmark.py performs real subprocess (Go build/run) and real network (gRPC, and optionally paid LLM API) calls when actually executed -- but this is gated behind explicit CLI invocation by a human/orchestrator, never imported/executed by any test or CI job by default.

test_run_live_benchmark.py exercises only pure-Python logic (retry policy, PipelineError mapping, path casing, cost-cap enforcement, judge-wrapping invariant) via mocks/monkeypatches -- zero real infra, zero real network, zero .env reads.

python-dotenv is a new third-party dependency; it is a small, widely-used, zero-transitive-dependency library with no known conflicts with existing pinned deps (fastapi/uvicorn/grpcio/pydantic/httpx/pymupdf).
